source_pr.title: executor: fix flaky test TestMemTracker4InsertAndReplaceExec
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #65220

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Fix flaky test TestMemTracker4InsertAndReplaceExec
```

symptoms: OOM/memory-tracker tests were flaky because the OOM capture message filter is shared state: if it isn't cleared between test sections, later assertions can observe stale filter behavior and miss/incorrectly capture tracker messages.
failure_signature: oomtest: intermittent assertion failures where OOM tracker/message capture does not match expectation due to stale message filter state
root_cause_explanation: The test uses an OOM capture helper with a mutable `messageFilter` to decide which log entries to record. That filter is shared state across the test’s steps (and potentially across tests if reused), so if it isn’t cleared, later steps can inherit stale filtering and behave nondeterministically. This PR fixes the flakiness by clearing the message filter before setting the expected filter for each step (`oom.ClearMessageFilter()`), and by treating an empty filter as “no filtering” so the capture logic is consistent.
fix_pattern: Reset/clear shared OOM-capture filter state between test steps (`ClearMessageFilter`) so later assertions don’t depend on stale global filter configuration; make empty-filter semantics consistent.

evidence: evidence: executor: fix flaky test TestMemTracker4InsertAndReplaceExec
